revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23985667504|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[en:Hida Gokoku Shrine]][[fr:Hida Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:飛騨護國神社]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Hida Gokoku Shrine
飛騨護國神社 | image = Hida-gokoku-jinja haiden.jpeg | caption = Hida Gokoku Shrine's ''[[haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|27.14|N|137|15|47.44|E|source:jawiki_region:JP_scale:10000|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = | founded_by = | established = 1909 | date_destroyed = | location = 90 Horibata-machi, {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|qid=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}
{{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}
Japan {{flagicon|Japan}} | website = | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Hida Gokoku Shrine'''|飛騨護國神社|Hida Gokoku Jinja}} is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|qid=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The shrine is designated to the people from the area who died during wars. The time period represented by the dead ranges from the {{ill|Satsuma Rebellion|en|Satsuma Rebellion|qid=Q714820|lt=Satsuma Rebellion|1=Satsuma Rebellion}} in 1877 to {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}. Because it represented only a small portion of the prefecture, it was eventually replaced by the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]]. ==History== The shrine was originally named '''Hida Shōkonsha''' (飛騨招魂社). In 1940, the [[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] was completed and began to represent the war dead for the entire prefecture. The shrine's festival days are May 2–3 and November 4–5. ==See also== *[[Gifu Gokoku Shrine]] *[[Nōhi Gokoku Shrine]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[en:Hida Gokoku Shrine]] {{Commons category|Hida-Gokoku-jinja}} [[fr:Hida Gokoku-jinja]] [[ja:飛騨護國神社]] {{wikidata link|Q3135218}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
